```{r}
# ############################################################################
#  IBS PATIENT SUBGROUPS â€” FINAL ANALYSIS PIPELINE
#  Andrew Seo | STAR study | LPA on baseline + EMA
#
#  HOW TO READ THIS FILE (for teammates):
#    It runs top to bottom in 5 parts. Each part answers ONE question.
#    Every result print starts with "-->" telling you what it means.
#
#    PART 1  Which variables, and why?            (finalize the inputs)
#    PART 2  What are the subgroups?              (run the clustering)
#    PART 3  Can we trust the 2-group split?      (validate it)
#    PART 4  Is it a NEW axis?                    (independent of known labels)
#    PART 5  Does it matter clinically?           (predict treatment response)
#
#  ONE RULE throughout: variables are continuous, standardized (scale()),
#  NEVER discretized. Clustering = latent profile analysis via mclust.
# ############################################################################

pkgs <- c("dplyr","mclust","tidyr","cluster")
for (p in pkgs) if (!requireNamespace(p, quietly=TRUE))
  install.packages(p, repos="https://cloud.r-project.org")
library(dplyr); library(mclust)

base_path <- "/Users/seobeomjin/Desktop/SURA IBS Clustering/"
wide <- read.csv(paste0(base_path, "wide_assign.csv"))   # 1 row per patient (baseline + outcomes)
ema  <- read.csv(paste0(base_path, "EMA_B1_SURA.csv"))    # many rows per patient (phone surveys)


# ############################################################################
#  PART 1 â€” WHICH VARIABLES, AND WHY?
#  We use 9 variables: 3 baseline questionnaires + 6 EMA features.
#  WHY these: factor analysis (done previously) showed IBS patients vary along
#  psychological distress, momentary affect, and IBS symptoms. These 9 cover
#  those axes. NOTE: 'distress' here is the EMA item "my IBS symptoms cause me
#  distress" -> it is a SYMPTOM measure, not a mood measure. We label it as such.
# ############################################################################
cat("\n############ PART 1: VARIABLES ############\n")

# Collapse EMA (many rows/patient) to ONE row/patient: mean = usual level,
# SD = day-to-day swing. Keep patients with >=10 responses (reliable summary).
ema_feat <- ema %>%
  group_by(PID) %>% filter(n() >= 10) %>%
  summarise(
    neg_mean      = mean(negative_beep, na.rm=TRUE),  neg_sd      = sd(negative_beep, na.rm=TRUE),
    stress_mean   = mean(stress,        na.rm=TRUE),  stress_sd   = sd(stress,        na.rm=TRUE),
    distress_mean = mean(distress,      na.rm=TRUE),  distress_sd = sd(distress,      na.rm=TRUE),
    .groups="drop")

# the 9 final variables, grouped by what they measure
final_vars <- c("BSI_Total_T1",        # baseline: psychological distress  (PSYCH)
                "SSS_Sum_T1_Recode",   # baseline: IBS symptom severity     (SYMPTOM)
                "VSI_Sum_T1",          # baseline: visceral/GI anxiety       (ANXIETY)
                "neg_mean","neg_sd",       # EMA: negative affect level + swing   (AFFECT)
                "stress_mean","stress_sd", # EMA: stress level + swing            (AFFECT)
                "distress_mean","distress_sd") # EMA: symptom-distress level + swing (SYMPTOM)

# build the analysis table: one row per patient, only complete cases
dat <- wide %>%
  select(PID, BSI_Total_T1, SSS_Sum_T1_Recode, VSI_Sum_T1) %>%
  left_join(ema_feat, by="PID") %>%
  select(PID, all_of(final_vars)) %>%
  na.omit()

cat(sprintf("--> Analysis sample: %d patients, %d variables.\n", nrow(dat), length(final_vars)))
cat("--> 3 baseline (BSI/SSS/VSI) + 6 EMA (neg, stress, symptom-distress; mean+SD)\n")


# ############################################################################
#  PART 2 â€” WHAT ARE THE SUBGROUPS?
#  Standardize, then let BIC pick the number of clusters K from 2..6.
# ############################################################################
cat("\n############ PART 2: CLUSTERING ############\n")

X <- scale(dat[, final_vars])     # standardize: mean 0, SD 1 (no discretizing)
set.seed(42)                      # reproducible
fit <- Mclust(X, G = 2:6)         # latent profile analysis; BIC chooses K

dat$cluster <- fit$classification
cat(sprintf("--> BIC selected K = %d clusters. Sizes: %s\n",
            fit$G, paste(table(dat$cluster), collapse=" / ")))

# show BIC per K so readers see WHY K was chosen (higher = better in mclust)
bic_by_k <- sapply(2:6, function(k) round(max(fit$BIC[as.character(k), ], na.rm=TRUE), 1))
names(bic_by_k) <- paste0("K=", 2:6)
cat("--> BIC by K (higher is better; it peaks at the chosen K):\n")
print(bic_by_k)

# name the clusters by their symptom severity so labels are meaningful
# (use simple one-word labels: lm coefficient names break with colons/spaces)
sev_by_cluster <- tapply(dat$SSS_Sum_T1_Recode, dat$cluster, mean)
high_cluster <- as.integer(names(which.max(sev_by_cluster)))
dat$Cluster <- factor(ifelse(dat$cluster == high_cluster, "HighBurden", "Resilient"),
                      levels = c("Resilient","HighBurden"))  # Resilient = reference
cat(sprintf("--> Cluster %d has higher symptom severity -> labeled 'HighBurden'.\n", high_cluster))


# ############################################################################
#  PART 3 â€” CAN WE TRUST THE SPLIT? (what separates the groups)
#  Standardized means per cluster: the biggest gaps show WHICH variables drive
#  the split. (Previously found: affect leads, symptom-distress supports.)
# ############################################################################
cat("\n############ PART 3: WHAT SEPARATES THE GROUPS ############\n")

zprof <- aggregate(X, list(cluster=dat$cluster), mean)
zt <- t(round(zprof[, -1], 2))
colnames(zt) <- paste0("clust", zprof$cluster)
zt <- cbind(zt, gap = round(abs(zt[, 1] - zt[, 2]), 2))
zt <- zt[order(-zt[, "gap"]), ]   # biggest separators on top
cat("--> Standardized cluster means, sorted by separation power (gap):\n")
print(zt)
cat("--> Variables at the top separate the groups most.\n")

# also report classification confidence (how sure the model is per patient)
cat(sprintf("--> Mean classification uncertainty: %.3f (near 0 = confident).\n",
            mean(fit$uncertainty)))

# silhouette: how geometrically separated are the clusters? (honesty check)
# ASW near 0 = clusters overlap (a spectrum), not sharply separated blocks.
if (requireNamespace("cluster", quietly=TRUE)) {
  sil <- cluster::silhouette(dat$cluster, dist(X))
  asw <- mean(sil[, "sil_width"])
  cat(sprintf("--> Average silhouette width (ASW) = %.3f\n", asw))
  cat("    (ASW ~0.2 means weak separation: IBS burden is a SPECTRUM, not\n")
  cat("     sharp categories. The 2 profiles summarize a gradient. We report\n")
  cat("     this honestly rather than overclaiming clean clusters.)\n")
}


# ############################################################################
#  PART 4 â€” IS IT A NEW AXIS? (independence from known labels)
#  A grouping is only useful if it adds something the existing labels don't.
#  We check two known labels: Rome IV bowel-habit subtype, and treatment arm.
# ############################################################################
cat("\n############ PART 4: INDEPENDENCE CHECKS ############\n")

# attach the known labels
dat$RomeIV    <- factor(wide$IBS_type[match(dat$PID, wide$PID)],
                        levels=c(1,2,3), labels=c("IBS-C","IBS-D","IBS-M"))
dat$Condition <- factor(wide$Condition[match(dat$PID, wide$PID)])

# (a) Rome IV subtype: distribution within each cluster + agreement (ARI)
cat("\n-- (a) vs Rome IV subtype --\n")
cat("--> % of each bowel-habit subtype within each cluster:\n")
print(round(prop.table(table(dat$Cluster, dat$RomeIV), 1) * 100, 1))
ari_rome <- adjustedRandIndex(dat$cluster,
              as.integer(wide$IBS_type[match(dat$PID, wide$PID)]))
cat(sprintf("--> ARI vs Rome IV = %.3f  (near 0 = independent of bowel-habit type)\n", ari_rome))

# (b) treatment arm: does the cluster just re-discover who got which treatment?
cat("\n-- (b) vs Treatment arm --\n")
cat("--> Treatment arm (CT/MA/MO) count within each cluster:\n")
print(table(Cluster = dat$Cluster, Condition = dat$Condition))
cat("--> If every arm appears in every cluster -> clusters are NOT treatment arm.\n")


# ############################################################################
#  PART 5 â€” DOES IT MATTER CLINICALLY? (treatment response)  â˜… THE KEY TEST
#  Question: do our baseline subgroups predict how symptoms change after
#  treatment? Outcome = IBS symptom severity (SSS) at T1, T2, T3.
#  Lower SSS = better. We test whether cluster predicts T3 AFTER adjusting for
#  baseline severity (so it's not just "they started different").
# ############################################################################
cat("\n############ PART 5: TREATMENT RESPONSE (the 'so what') ############\n")

# attach the three time points of symptom severity
dat$SSS_T1 <- wide$SSS_Sum_T1_Recode[match(dat$PID, wide$PID)]
dat$SSS_T2 <- wide$SSS_Sum_T2_Recode[match(dat$PID, wide$PID)]
dat$SSS_T3 <- wide$SSS_Sum_T3_Recode[match(dat$PID, wide$PID)]

# (a) descriptive: mean SSS over time, per cluster
cat("\n-- (a) Symptom severity (SSS) over time, by cluster --\n")
traj <- dat %>% group_by(Cluster) %>%
  summarise(n=n(),
            T1=round(mean(SSS_T1, na.rm=TRUE),1),
            T2=round(mean(SSS_T2, na.rm=TRUE),1),
            T3=round(mean(SSS_T3, na.rm=TRUE),1),
            drop_T1_T3=round(mean(SSS_T1,na.rm=TRUE)-mean(SSS_T3,na.rm=TRUE),1),
            pct_drop=round(100*(mean(SSS_T1,na.rm=TRUE)-mean(SSS_T3,na.rm=TRUE))/
                           mean(SSS_T1,na.rm=TRUE),1),
            .groups="drop")
print(as.data.frame(traj))
cat("--> Lower SSS = better. Compare absolute drop AND % drop between clusters.\n")

# (b) THE TEST: does cluster predict T3 after adjusting for baseline (T1)?
#     If the 'Cluster' line is significant (p<0.05), the grouping predicts
#     outcome beyond just starting severity = clinically meaningful.
cat("\n-- (b) Baseline-adjusted test: does cluster predict outcome? --\n")
m_adj <- lm(SSS_T3 ~ SSS_T1 + Cluster, data = dat)
cat(sprintf("--> Model uses %d patients with complete T1+T3 data.\n",
            length(m_adj$residuals)))
print(round(summary(m_adj)$coefficients, 4))
p_cluster <- summary(m_adj)$coefficients["ClusterHighBurden", "Pr(>|t|)"]
cat(sprintf("\n--> Cluster p-value = %.4f\n", p_cluster))
if (p_cluster < 0.05) {
  cat("--> SIGNIFICANT: clusters predict treatment outcome beyond baseline.\n")
  cat("    The subgroups are PROGNOSTIC (clinically meaningful).\n")
} else {
  cat("--> NOT significant: once baseline is matched, clusters don't predict T3.\n")
  cat("    The subgroups are DESCRIPTIVE, not prognostic. (Report honestly.)\n")
}
cat("    CAVEAT: baseline SSS_T1 is also one of the 9 clustering variables, so\n")
cat("    cluster and SSS_T1 share some information. The adjustment is still\n")
cat("    valid (clusters use 9 variables, not just SSS), but this mild overlap\n")
cat("    should be acknowledged. This is prediction, not causation.\n")

# (c) does treatment arm interact with cluster? (does one arm help one group more?)
cat("\n-- (c) Does treatment arm work differently by cluster? (interaction) --\n")
m_int <- lm(SSS_T3 ~ SSS_T1 + Cluster * Condition, data = dat)
cat(sprintf("--> Interaction model R-squared = %.3f\n", summary(m_int)$r.squared))
cat("--> (See Cluster:Condition rows below; significant = arm effect differs by group)\n")
print(round(summary(m_int)$coefficients, 4))


# ############################################################################
#  SUMMARY (printed)
# ############################################################################
cat("\n############ ONE-PARAGRAPH SUMMARY ############\n")
cat(sprintf(
"We clustered %d IBS patients on 9 baseline+EMA variables (LPA). BIC selected
K=%d: a high-burden minority and a resilient majority. The split is driven
mainly by momentary affect, with symptom-distress contributing. Clusters are
independent of Rome IV subtype (ARI=%.2f) and treatment arm. The key test of
clinical value is the baseline-adjusted model in Part 5(b): cluster p=%.3f.\n",
nrow(dat), fit$G, ari_rome, p_cluster))
```
